title: Huygens Probe Touches Down on Titan
story: Imagine exploring an alien world! In 2005, the Huygens probe did just that. It landed on Titan, Saturn's largest moon, on January 14. This brave little spacecraft sent back amazing pictures of Titan's surface. Scientists were thrilled to see rocky ground and possible liquid methane lakes. It was the first time humans had glimpsed the mysterious surface of this distant moon.
activity_question: What did the Huygens probe land on?
bonus_fact: Titan is the only moon in our solar system known to have a thick atmosphere.

title: Giant Ice Disk Spins in Maine River
story: A massive ice disk appeared in a Maine river, spinning like a giant frozen carousel. On January 14, 2019, residents of Westbrook spotted the circular ice formation in the Presumpscot River. Measuring about 300 meters wide, the disk looked like a miniature moon floating on water. Its slow rotation mesmerized onlookers and quickly became a local attraction, drawing curious visitors to witness this rare natural phenomenon.
activity_question: What did the ice disk in the Presumpscot River resemble?
follow_up_question: How do you think such a large, circular ice formation might form naturally in a river?

title: The Inventor Who Helped Us See Inside
story: John Mallard was born in Northampton, England in 1927 on January 14. This brilliant medical physicist would go on to change the way doctors look inside our bodies. These incredible inventions allow doctors to see detailed pictures of our organs and tissues, helping them spot problems and save lives.

title: Roy Castle's Lightning-Fast Feet
story: Tap, tap, tap! Roy Castle's feet moved faster than the eye could see. This talented dancer amazed everyone with his incredible speed. On January 14, 1973, Castle showed off his skills on BBC TV. His tapping was so quick, it was measured at a whopping 1,440 taps per minute! That's like tapping your foot 24 times every second. Castle's fancy footwork left viewers in awe of his remarkable talent.

The clarinet's unique voice could play both soft melodies and lively tunes.

title: Conquering the Roof of the Americas

He not only wrote moving poems but also founded Broadside Press, a groundbreaking publishing house that amplified Black voices in literature.
activity_question: What did Dudley Randall found?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for diverse voices to be heard in literature?

title: Zorro's Star Born in the Big Apple
story: Swashbuckling hero Zorro leapt onto TV screens thanks to Guy Williams, born in New York City in 1924 on January 14. This dashing actor, whose real name was Armando Joseph Catalano, became famous for his roles in the Zorro series and as the space-faring dad in Lost in Space. Williams brought adventure to life for a generation of viewers.
activity_question: What was Guy Williams' real name?
follow_up_question: Besides Zorro, what other famous TV show did Guy Williams star in?
